markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Rainbow Six Siege Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between 9z and SuperNova Team in the South America League Challenger Series Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 12 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if 9z and SuperNova Team play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: O/U 2.5 Games

events.title: Rainbow Six Siege: 9z vs SuperNova Team (BO3) - South America League Challenger Series Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Rainbow Six Siege Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between 9z and SuperNova Team in the South America League Challenger Series Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 12 at 5:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "9z" if 9z win the match against SuperNova Team.

This market will resolve to "SuperNova Team" if SuperNova Team win the match against 9z.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/rainbowsix/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Blackburn Rovers FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Blackburn Rovers FC and West Bromwich Albion FC, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Blackburn Rovers FC and West Bromwich Albion FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Blackburn Rovers FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Blackburn Rovers FC and West Bromwich Albion FC, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Blackburn Rovers FC and West Bromwich Albion FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. markets.question: Medvedev vs. Michelsen: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Daniil Medvedev and Alex Michelsen in the BNP Paribas Open, scheduled for March 11 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
markets.groupItemTitle: BNP Paribas Open: Daniil Medvedev vs Alex Michelsen Set 1 O/U 8.5

events.title: BNP Paribas Open: Daniil Medvedev vs Alex Michelsen
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Daniil Medvedev and Alex Michelsen in the BNP Paribas Open, scheduled for March 11 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Daniil Medvedev' if Daniil Medvedev advances against Alex Michelsen.

This market will resolve to 'Alex Michelsen' if Alex Michelsen advances against Daniil Medvedev.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Spread: Sheffield United FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Sheffield United FC" if Sheffield United FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Bristol City FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
